notes: Bronze DQ rules for IPEDS Finance Survey (F1A / F2 / F3) joined with EFIA (12-Month Instructional Activity), filtered to ICLEVEL=1 AND HLOFFER>=5 against HD2023, single-vintage fiscal_year=2023. Source spec: docs/specs/full-pipeline-ipeds-finance.md §4 (rules RAW-IPF-001..RAW-IPF-014). EDA at governance/eda/full-pipeline-ipeds-finance-raw-eda.md observed 2,675 post-filter rows; form mix F1A 30.6% / F2 59.0% / F3 10.4%; FTE non-null 97.94%; F3 endowment 100% structurally NULL (no F3H family); F3 institutional support 0% NULL on FY23 (post-2014-15 schedule populated, refutes pre-v1.3 hypothesis); imputation <=1.22% across all four analytical fields. RAW-IPF-001 row-count band recalibrated from spec-as-written 5,000-8,000 to 2,500-3,200 per EDA §0 gate 6: the 5,000-8,000 band predates the HD-filter narrowing (ICLEVEL=1 AND HLOFFER>=5). All other thresholds (RAW-IPF-009/010/011/012 at 90/90/95/60) retained as written per spec; EDA §12 explicitly recommends keeping these because observed values (100%/100%/97.94%/76.0%) sit comfortably above the floors with margin. RAW-IPF-007 (endowment_value >= 0) is written as 'where non-null' so it does not trip on the 100% structural NULL on F3 rows; F3's 277 NULL endowment values are correct behavior, not violations.

rules.name: Row count between 2,500 and 3,200
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM bronze.ipeds_finance) BETWEEN 2500 AND 3200 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Post-HD-filter (ICLEVEL=1 AND HLOFFER>=5) finance UNION across F1A/F2/F3 should land between 2,500 and 3,200 institutions per fiscal year. Below 2,500 means the HD-filter or finance-form fetch dropped rows; above 3,200 means the HD-filter weakened or a non-finance file leaked in.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-IPF-001 row count between 5,000 and 8,000 (P0, Volume)' RECALIBRATED to 2,500-3,200 per EDA §0 gate 6 and §12: 'Post-filter row count actual = 2,675 -- well below the 5,000 floor. The rule was sized for the unfiltered finance UNION (~6,500 institutions). After applying ICLEVEL=1 AND HLOFFER>=5, only 2,675 rows survive.' EDA §6: 'Pre-filter: 6,163 UNITIDs in HD2023. Post-filter: 2,864 UNITIDs match. Post-join with finance forms: 2,675 rows survive.' Threshold: count in [2500, 3200], gives ~6% headroom below observed (margin to 2,500) and ~20% headroom above (3,200 cap absorbs a year with weaker HD attrition).

rules.name: unitid non-null (100%)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.ipeds_finance WHERE unitid IS NULL
rules.description: unitid is the natural key of the IPEDS Finance Survey (and the FK to every IPEDS-keyed downstream table including HD, EFIA, College Scorecard, EADA). Null means a coercion failure in the ingestor's column-coalescing path or an upstream finance-form CSV corruption.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-IPF-002 unitid non-null 100% (P0, Completeness)'. EDA §6 reports 2,675 post-filter rows; the dedup grain is [unitid] which presupposes non-null unitid throughout. EDA spot-checked 5 institutions with explicit UNITIDs (110635/139959/199193/243744/151351) -- all present. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: unitid uniqueness (dedup grain)
rules.sql: SELECT unitid, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM bronze.ipeds_finance GROUP BY unitid HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
rules.description: unitid is the declared dedup grain of bronze.ipeds_finance (one row per institution per fiscal year, single-vintage scope). Any duplicate indicates a UNION-then-LEFT-JOIN-EFIA failure (e.g., an institution appearing on two forms in the same fiscal year due to mid-year sector conversion), an EFIA fan-out bug (EFIA2023 grain is 5,959/5,959 unique so no fan-out is expected), or a double-append to the Iceberg table.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-IPF-003 unitid uniqueness (P0, Uniqueness)'. Spec §4: 'Grain: UNITID per fiscal year. Dedup grain: [unitid].' EDA §2 EFIA2023 verification: 'Rows: 5,959 / Distinct UNITIDs: 5,959 (one-row-per-UNITID grain -- NO dedup filter required).' EDA §7 form mix: 819 + 1,579 + 277 = 2,675 = post-filter row count, confirming no inter-form duplicates. Threshold: 0 duplicate unitid values.

rules.name: report_form in {F1A, F2, F3}
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.ipeds_finance WHERE report_form NOT IN ('F1A', 'F2', 'F3')
rules.description: report_form is the form-of-origin tag stamped by the UNION ingestor: F1A (public, GASB), F2 (private nonprofit, FASB), or F3 (for-profit). Any other value indicates ingestor logic drift (e.g., an unhandled F1B or F1A_F2 hybrid form) or a label coercion bug.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-IPF-004 report_form in {F1A, F2, F3} (P0, Validity)'. Spec §3 column table enumerates exactly three forms. EDA §7 form mix table: 'F1A 819 / F2 1,579 / F3 277' -- exactly three distinct values across 2,675 rows. Threshold: 0 rows with out-of-domain report_form.

rules.name: instruction_expenses >= 0 where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.ipeds_finance WHERE instruction_expenses IS NOT NULL AND instruction_expenses < 0
rules.description: Instruction expenses are a non-negative monetary amount by definition. Negative values are impossible in the IPEDS Finance reporting framework; any negative value indicates an IPEDS suppression sentinel (-1, -2, '.') that escaped the ingestor's null-coercion path. The 5 observed rows with instruction_expenses = $0 (small F2/F3 institutions booking teaching costs under academic support) are real reported zeros and pass this rule.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-IPF-005 instruction_expenses >= 0 where non-null (P0, Validity)'. EDA §5 distribution table: 'instruction_expenses min = $0' (n=5 rows at zero, all small religious or proprietary schools). EDA §11 anomalies: 'instruction_expenses =0: 5, LOW, small F2/F3 institutions; spec §4 RAW-IPF-005 explicitly allows >=0.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: institutional_support_expenses >= 0 where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.ipeds_finance WHERE institutional_support_expenses IS NOT NULL AND institutional_support_expenses < 0
rules.description: Institutional support expenses are non-negative by definition. Negative values indicate an IPEDS suppression sentinel that escaped the ingestor's null-coercion path. The 17 observed rows with institutional_support_expenses = $0 (mostly F2 single-campus seminaries) are real reported zeros and pass this rule.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-IPF-006 institutional_support_expenses >= 0 where non-null (P0, Validity)'. EDA §5 distribution: 'institutional_support_expenses min = $0.' EDA §11 anomalies: 'institutional_support_expenses =0: 17, LOW, mostly F2 single-campus seminaries; allowed.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: endowment_value >= 0 where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.ipeds_finance WHERE endowment_value IS NOT NULL AND endowment_value < 0
rules.description: Endowment value is non-negative by definition; per IPEDS reporting, underwater funds report 0, never negative. The 'WHERE endowment_value IS NOT NULL' clause is structurally important: F3 (for-profit) institutions have NO F3H family on their finance schedule, so endowment_value is NULL for 100% of F3 rows (277 / 277). These NULLs are correct behavior, not violations -- the F3 filter via IS NOT NULL prevents this rule from tripping on the structural absence. The 128 rows with endowment_value = $0 (small institutions and underwater funds) are real reported zeros and pass this rule.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-IPF-007 endowment_value >= 0 where non-null (P0, Validity, underwater funds report 0, never negative).' EDA §5 distribution: 'endowment_value min = $0; non-null 76.0% (2,033/2,675).' EDA §7 F3 NULL rates: 'endowment_value: 277 NULL / 100.0% -- expected. F3 has no F3H family.' EDA §11 anomalies: 'endowment_value =0 (where non-null): 128, LOW, underwater funds + small institutions; allowed by RAW-IPF-007.' EDA §3: 'F3 has no F3H family -- F3 endowment field correctly coalesces to NULL.' The 'where non-null' wording is critical and is preserved from the spec to ensure the rule does not trip on the 277 structural F3 NULLs. Threshold: 0 violations among non-null rows.

rules.name: total_fte_enrollment > 0 where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.ipeds_finance WHERE total_fte_enrollment IS NOT NULL AND total_fte_enrollment <= 0
rules.description: total_fte_enrollment is computed as NULL-safe COALESCE(FTEUG,0)+COALESCE(FTEGD,0)+COALESCE(FTEDPP,0) from EFIA2023. When non-null (i.e., at least one of the three components is reported), the sum must be strictly positive: an institution that exists in EFIA must have at least one student. A non-null zero is impossible by construction. A non-null negative is impossible because the FTE columns themselves are non-negative counts. Confirmed observed minimum is FTE=6 (SUNY Empire State College), well above zero.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-IPF-008 total_fte_enrollment > 0 where non-null (P0, Validity, hard).' EDA §5 distribution: 'total_fte_enrollment min = 6 (SUNY Empire State College @ UNITID 196097, FY23 transition year, mostly online graduate students). Verified plausible.' EDA §11 anomalies: 'fte_total =6 (min): 1, LOW, verified plausible.' Per-FTE division by zero would propagate to base/consumable layers, so this is a P0 hard rule. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: instruction_expenses non-null >= 90%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN instruction_expenses IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) FROM bronze.ipeds_finance) >= 0.90 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: instruction_expenses is a required reporting line on all three IPEDS finance forms (F1A F1C011 / F2 F2E011 / F3 F3E011). Observed completeness in FY23 is 100.0% (2,675 / 2,675). The 90% threshold is intentionally retained from the spec rather than tightened: the 10-point margin absorbs a future-cycle codebook revision or a partial finance-form release without triggering false alarms, while still binding hard if completeness collapses by an order of magnitude.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-IPF-009 instruction_expenses non-null >= 90% (P0, Completeness, hard).' EDA §5 distribution: 'instruction_expenses non-null %: 100.0% (2,675/2,675).' EDA §12 explicit recommendation: 'RAW-IPF-009: keep 90% (margin to 100% observed). Observed 100.00% -- rule passes.' Spec threshold preserved per EDA recommendation. Threshold: non-null fraction >= 0.90.

rules.name: institutional_support_expenses non-null >= 90%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN institutional_support_expenses IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) FROM bronze.ipeds_finance) >= 0.90 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: institutional_support_expenses is reported on F1A (F1C071), F2 (F2E061), and F3 (F3E03C1, post-2014-15 schedule). Observed completeness in FY23 is 100.0% (2,675 / 2,675), including 0% NULL on F3 -- the v1.3 EDA refuted the pre-v1.3 hypothesis that F3 omitted institutional support. The 90% spec threshold is preserved: it leaves headroom for a future F3 schedule revision that re-suppresses the field, while still binding hard if F3 institutional support drops out broadly.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-IPF-010 institutional_support_expenses non-null >= 90% (P0, Completeness, hard).' EDA §5 distribution: 'institutional_support_expenses non-null %: 100.0% (2,675/2,675).' EDA §7 F3 NULL rates: 'institutional_support_expenses: 0 NULL / 0.0% <- refutes the pre-v1.3 hypothesis. F3 IS reporting institutional support fully.' EDA §0 Executive Summary Req 6: 'F3 inst-support 0% NULL (post-2014-15 schedule populated, refutes pre-v1.3 hypothesis).' EDA §12 explicit recommendation: 'RAW-IPF-010: keep 90%. Observed 100.00% -- rule passes.' Spec threshold preserved per EDA recommendation. Threshold: non-null fraction >= 0.90.

rules.name: total_fte_enrollment non-null >= 95%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN total_fte_enrollment IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) FROM bronze.ipeds_finance) >= 0.95 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: total_fte_enrollment comes from the LEFT JOIN to EFIA2023; an institution can appear in finance forms but be absent from EFIA (newly-opened in FY23 or late filer). Observed completeness is 97.94% (2,620 / 2,675), narrowly above the 95% floor. The 95% threshold is preserved per spec (the EDA recommends not tightening because the 2.94-point margin is narrow but absorbs the natural year-to-year EFIA-finance join attrition). If observed completeness drops below ~96%, that is an EDA-finding-worthy regression to escalate.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-IPF-011 total_fte_enrollment non-null >= 95% (P0, Completeness, hard).' EDA §5 distribution: 'total_fte_enrollment non-null %: 97.94% (2,620/2,675).' EDA §7 F3 NULL rates: 'total_fte_enrollment: 4 NULL / 1.4%' on F3 (overall NULL across all forms is 55/2,675 = 2.06%). EDA §11 anomalies: 'total_fte_enrollment NULL: 55 (2.06%), INFO, UNITIDs in finance forms but absent from EFIA2023 (newly-opened in FY23 or late filers).' EDA §12 explicit recommendation: 'RAW-IPF-011: keep 95% (margin to 97.94%). Observed 97.94% -- narrow but passes; flag if drops below 96%.' Spec threshold preserved. Threshold: non-null fraction >= 0.95.

rules.name: endowment_value non-null >= 60%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN endowment_value IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) FROM bronze.ipeds_finance) >= 0.60 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: endowment_value is structurally absent for 100% of F3 (for-profit) rows because F3 has no F3H family on its finance schedule. F3 makes up 10.4% of the post-filter row population, contributing ~10 percentage points of guaranteed NULL. Additionally ~16% of F2 small private institutions don't maintain endowments. Observed non-null is 76.0% (2,033 / 2,675), comfortably above the 60% floor. P1 (rather than P0) reflects that the structural F3 NULL means a tightening of this rule would be a category error -- 60% is set deliberately below the F3 floor (1 - 0.104 = 0.896) plus reasonable F2 sparsity allowance.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-IPF-012 endowment_value non-null >= 60% (P1, Completeness, many small institutions report 0 or null).' EDA §5 distribution: 'endowment_value n=2,033, non-null %: 76.0%.' EDA §7 F3 NULL rates: 'endowment_value: 277 NULL / 100.0%' (structural). EDA §11 anomalies: 'endowment_value NULL: 642 (24.0%), INFO, concentrated in F3 (277 = 100% of F3) + ~16% of F2; structural.' EDA §12 explicit recommendation: 'RAW-IPF-012: keep 60% (76% observed). Observed 76.0% (24% NULL is mostly structural F3 + 16% small F2).' Spec threshold preserved per EDA recommendation. Threshold: non-null fraction >= 0.60.

rules.name: fiscal_year is single value across all rows
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT fiscal_year) FROM bronze.ipeds_finance) = 1 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Single-vintage invariant: the entire load must carry exactly one fiscal_year. This is the hackathon-scope decision (matches BEA RPP precedent) -- no SCD2, no multi-year stacking. The ingestor pins fiscal_year via the constructor argument (currently 2023 because NCES has not yet released FY24). Multiple distinct values means either two cycles were loaded into the same partition or the ingestor's pin logic broke. On annual refresh the value bumps in lockstep across the entire load.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-IPF-013 fiscal_year is single value across all rows (P0, Consistency, single-vintage invariant).' Spec §2 Decision 6 + ADV-3: 'Single-vintage scope is correct for hackathon. When SCD2 is later introduced, dedup grain must extend to [unitid, fiscal_year].' EDA §1 acquisition status pins the operative cycle: 'Run promote against FY23 (provisional). FY24 reverts to the same workflow when NCES publishes it.' EDA §0 gate 1: 'Fiscal-year flip: ingestor DEFAULT_FISCAL_YEAR = 2024. FY24 Finance is NOT yet released... Pin via IpedsFinanceIngestor(fiscal_year=2023, ...).' All 2,675 EDA-analyzed rows carry fiscal_year=2023. Threshold: exactly 1 distinct fiscal_year.

rules.name: Spot check: at least one row instruction_expenses > $100M (large R1 anchor)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM bronze.ipeds_finance WHERE instruction_expenses > 100000000) >= 1 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Sanity spot-check: every recent IPEDS Finance cycle has multiple R1 public and elite private institutions reporting >$100M in instruction expenses (UC Berkeley $1.0B, Stanford $2.7B, UMich, UCLA, Penn State, etc.). Zero such rows means either a unit error (dollars vs thousands), a column-coalescing failure that nulled out the largest reporters, or a fetch-truncation regression. P1 because this is a plausibility tripwire: the magnitude check catches the gross-anchor failure mode that pointwise validity rules cannot. Observed FY23 count is 365 rows above $100M (top-of-distribution P95 is $274M, P99 is $996M, max $3.5B Stanford).
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-IPF-014 spot check: at least one row with instruction_expenses > $100M (large R1 anchor) (P1, Plausibility, EDA-calibrated).' EDA §5 distribution: 'instruction_expenses P95 $274,546,660; P99 $995,734,601; max $3,504,073,000.' EDA §4 spot-check: 'UC Berkeley $1,002,622,163; Stanford $2,683,135,000; UNC-CH $561,419,293; UGA $439,309,622; IU-B $761,628,765 -- all > $100M.' EDA §12 calibration note: 'RAW-IPF-014 anchor: observed 365 rows; calibrate to >=200.' Per spec the rule is the >=1 floor; the EDA's >=200 suggestion is left as future tightening but not adopted here because it would diverge from spec wording without explicit spec amendment. Threshold: at least 1 row > $100,000,000.
